scenario.description: Upstream cloud provider DNS changes cause geographic misrouting of email traffic
scenario.long_description: Upstream cloud provider implemented DNS infrastructure changes that caused the dns-resolver-service to receive incorrect geographic routing information. Instead of returning US-based endpoints for MX record lookups, DNS responses directed traffic to non-US region endpoints (simulated by pointing the DNS resolver at an unreachable upstream, causing genuine resolution timeouts). This introduced significant latency into the message-router service, which cascaded to both outbound-delivery-service (email delivery delays) and inbound-processing-service (processing delays). As DNS lookups timed out and delivery attempts slowed, the queue-manager experienced increasing message backlog (visible as elevated redis.net.commands rate). This backpressure eventually affected the smtp-gateway, causing customer-visible SMTP connection delays. Root cause: dns-resolver-service UPSTREAM_DNS_HOST pointed at unreachable endpoint due to upstream provider DNS infrastructure change. Recovery occurred when the upstream DNS configuration was restored, allowing the dns-resolver-service to resume normal resolution.
